text: ‘Strong and vigorous, as a man of Benjamin should be’; literally, ‘son of a man of Jemini, strong and vigorous’. The Latin version treats the words ‘son of a man of Jemini’ as if they were part of the genealogy; but a descent so traced from a nameless ancestor is both unparalleled and improbable. The Hebrew text may bear the sense ‘a man of great wealth’, rather than ‘strong and vigorous’.
